features.name: Daily Instagram + trend scraper pipeline
features.goal: Scrape the 6-influencer Instagram roster and 2 trend-report sources daily, persisting snapshots to Supabase.
features.why_it_matters: Foundation for metrics, dashboards, and recommendations — nothing downstream works without reliable daily data.
non_goals: No recommendations generation (Phase 2)
No web platform/dashboard (Phase 3)
No influencer-brand matching
No cloud-hosted scheduler, launchd only
No private-account access
acceptance_criteria: Supabase schema matches scraper/schema.sql (influencers, profile_snapshots, post_snapshots, trend_snapshots, recommendations)
Manual scraper run produces profile_snapshots + post_snapshots rows for all 6 roster handles
Trend scraper produces one trend_snapshots row per source per day, idempotent same-day re-run
A broken influencer handle logs and skips without aborting the run
A broken trend source logs and skips without aborting the run
Config lives in .env / influencers.txt, never hardcoded
launchd LaunchAgent plist runs the pipeline once daily and skips same-day re-runs
pytest suite passes for scraper pure-logic (metrics, idempotency guard, error-skip)
features.notes: 24/24 pytest passing with mocks (config guard, error-skip behavior). Not yet run against real Instagram/Supabase (blocked on user creating a Supabase project). Spec file: agent-harness/spec.md.

features.name: Metrics + Gemini creative recommendations
features.goal: Compute rule-based engagement/growth/format/cadence metrics per influencer, then generate a personalized Gemini recommendation grounded in those metrics plus the latest trend_snapshots.
features.why_it_matters: Raw scraped rows aren't useful on their own; the agency and talent need metrics summarized and creative recommendations that reference real trend context, not generic advice.
non_goals: No dashboard UI (Phase 3)
No influencer-brand matching
No recommendations without at least one profile_snapshots history point (need deltas)
acceptance_criteria: metrics.py computes engagement rate, follower delta, format performance breakdown, posting cadence from snapshot rows, covered by pytest with fixed input/output
recommendations.py builds a Gemini prompt from an influencer's metrics + recent posts + latest trend_snapshots, in Spanish and English
Recommendation text stored in recommendations table with model name and timestamp
run_daily.py calls recommendation generation once per influencer after the scrape, skipping gracefully if Gemini call fails for one influencer
Gemini API key read from env, never hardcoded
features.notes: 24/24 pytest passing with mocks. Not yet run against real Gemini (blocked on Google API key). Spec captured inline in this entry rather than a separate spec.md revision, per user's request to move quickly through phases.

features.name: Password-gated Next.js platform (roster, influencer dashboard, trends)
features.goal: Give agency staff and talent a hosted, password-gated web platform to view roster metrics, per-influencer dashboards with follower growth and creative recommendations, and trend reports.
features.why_it_matters: Raw data and recommendations sitting only in Supabase aren't accessible to non-technical staff or the talent themselves; a simple shared-password site makes the pipeline's output usable day to day.
non_goals: No per-user accounts / email login
No influencer-brand matching
No editing/writing back to Supabase from the platform (read-only)
acceptance_criteria: Single shared password (LAYCC via SITE_PASSWORD env) gates every route except /login, enforced in proxy.ts
Session cookie is HMAC-signed with SESSION_SECRET, httpOnly, not forgeable without the secret
Roster page lists all influencers with latest follower count and delta
Per-influencer page shows follower growth chart, engagement rate, format performance, and latest recommendation
Trends page lists latest scraped trend_snapshots
Supabase service key only used in server-only modules, never sent to the client
next build/tsc/eslint all pass
features.notes: Built ahead of its own Planner spec at the user's explicit request to move quickly through all phases in one session. Verified manually via Playwright MCP browser driving (login/logout/wrong-password/404/session-persistence all confirmed) rather than a checked-in Playwright test suite — see findings.md Missing Requirements.

features.name: On-brand, Spanish-market creative recommendations
features.goal: Tailor each influencer's Gemini recommendations to their persona (elite presenter, comedian, MotoGP athlete, ...) and ground them in Spanish-market trend sources instead of US-centric ones.
features.why_it_matters: Recommendations currently give every influencer the same trend context from US-leaning sources; the agency's audience is Spain and each talent has a distinct brand that generic advice violates.
non_goals: No JS-rendered trend sources (requests + BeautifulSoup only)
No new source types (APIs/RSS) — URL scraping only
No platform UI for viewing/editing persona
No profile images (separate upcoming spec)
No per-influencer trend-source targeting
acceptance_criteria: config.TREND_SOURCES holds exactly the 4 Spanish-market URLs from spec.md (newengen.com removed, modash.io kept, garajedoce/lagahe/venizecomunicacion added) — done
influencers table has nullable persona text column (schema.sql + live alter table), seeded for all 5 roster handles per spec.md — done

build_prompt includes persona + Spain/Spanish-audience instruction, adapts trends to persona rather than forcing off-brand formats, keeps bilingual output, and degrades gracefully when persona is null — done
run_daily.py/db.py pass persona from the influencer row into generate_recommendation — done
pytest covers persona-in-prompt, Spain-audience-in-prompt, null-persona fallback, and len(TREND_SOURCES) == 4; all existing tests pass — done, 31/31

One live run_daily produces 4 trend_snapshots rows and at least one persona-reflecting recommendation — done: 3 new sources scraped live (modash already scraped that day), antonlofer recommendation generated and stored referencing the new sources by name and staying on-persona
features.notes: Spec: agent-harness/spec.md (rewritten for this change 2026-07-06). All 3 new source URLs verified scrapable with the existing scrape_trend_source on 2026-07-06. Personas sourced from user input plus public research. Live-verified end to end same session; no separate Evaluator subagent pass run (see contract.md note on Evaluator Independence, same pattern as feature_003).

features.name: Deep backfill, view counts, deterministic highlights, stale-roster cleanup
features.goal: One-time 36-post-per-influencer backfill to build a baseline, capture video/reel view counts, compute deterministic per-influencer highlights (dashboard + Gemini prompt), and delete the stale cristinapedroche impostor row.
features.why_it_matters: 12 recent posts with no views and current-batch-only metrics can't produce eye-catching insights like 'this post performed 100% better than their typical post'; and a 41-follower impostor account is showing on the roster.
non_goals: No change to the daily post cap (POSTS_PER_INFLUENCER stays 12)
No change to scrape frequency (backfill is a one-off manual run)
No per-user auth, no influencer-brand matching
No LLM-generated insights (highlights are deterministic Python)
No platform UI for editing highlights
No paid scraping API migration
acceptance_criteria: cristinapedroche influencers row + snapshot children deleted from live Supabase; exactly the 5 correct handles remain active
post_snapshots has nullable views column (schema.sql + live ALTER TABLE); scraper captures post.video_view_count defensively for videos/reels; db.insert_post_snapshots writes it
python -m youfirst_scraper.backfill scrapes 36 posts/influencer through existing paths, 20s delay, no .last_run touch; daily cap unchanged at 12

metrics.py gains pure functions: per-post outperformance vs median, per-post growth vs closest capture >=6 days older (graceful when no history), follower week-over-week growth, top reel by views vs median reel views, and compute_highlights emitting only notable (|delta| >= 30%) human-readable strings
highlights table (id, influencer_id, content, metric jsonb, captured_at) written by backfill and daily runs; build_prompt includes highlights and leads with the standout stat
platform data.ts fetches latest highlights; influencer page renders a Highlights section in existing Tailwind style
Backfill executed against real Instagram/Supabase (36 x 5), recommendations regenerated after; throttling reported honestly
pytest green in scraper/ with extended test_metrics.py + updated test_recommendations.py (external calls mocked)
Dev server check: 5-handle roster, cristipedroche ~2-3M followers, Highlights section renders
features.notes: Spec: agent-harness/spec.md (rewritten for this change 2026-07-06). Backfill size (36), highlight semantics, hard-delete of the stale row, and full harness process confirmed with the user 2026-07-06.

features.name: Dashboard metrics & UX overhaul
features.goal: Fix confusing dashboard metrics, add navigation and post-level context, hide follower chart until enough history exists, and deduplicate post snapshots for accurate metrics.
features.why_it_matters: Agency staff cannot trust or act on raw avg-engagement floats, missing back navigation, day-one flat charts, or metrics corrupted by duplicate post snapshots.
non_goals: No follower demographics (gender/location)
No scraper or schema changes
No third-party audience analytics APIs
No Trends page or auth changes
acceptance_criteria: getInfluencerDashboard deduplicates post_snapshots by shortcode (latest capture wins, up to 12 posts)
Per-format cards show engagement rate %, formatted avg interactions, avg reel views, and sample size
Influencer page has back link to roster; bio removed
Follower chart aggregates one point per day; hidden until 3+ days with progress note; K/M Y-axis
Recent posts table with date, format, likes, comments, views, engagement rate, Instagram link
Stat row: Followers, Change (timeframe label), Engagement rate (subtext), Posting cadence
Recommendations rendered as styled markdown
Roster uses shared formatter and clearer delta labeling
Playwright tests pass; platform build/tsc/eslint pass
features.notes: Spec: agent-harness/spec.md (rewritten 2026-07-06). User confirmed post-level metrics over demographics and hide-until-ready chart.

features.name: Content-grounded recommendation bullets + roster attention alerts
features.goal: Analyze top-performing posts' video/audio with Gemini so recommendations can cite what a reel was actually about, rewrite recommendations as short Spanish-only data-grounded bullets, and surface engagement-drop/posting-gap alerts on the roster.
features.why_it_matters: Recommendations today only see recent captions and generic trend articles, so they can't say 'your reel about X performed well, do a follow-up.' Bilingual prose output is also too dense for low-patience readers, and daily-scraped performance signals have no roster-level surface.
non_goals: No email/push alert digest
No comparable-creator scraping
No removal of the trend-scraper module, only unhooked from the recommendation prompt
No language toggle - Spanish only
No third-party scraping API (Apify) migration - fetch/analyze kept separate for a future swap, not built now
acceptance_criteria: content_analysis.py downloads video/thumbnail for new posts at/above median engagement (capped ~4/run/influencer), sends to Gemini for Spanish summary/topic/format/hook, stores once per shortcode in post_content table, skips gracefully on failure
post_content table added to schema.sql
recommendations.build_prompt cites top performers with content summaries and multiple-of-median stats plus a weakest-post contrast; trend-report section removed
generate_recommendation requests Gemini JSON response mode, returns {bullets:[{text,reason,shortcode}]}, 3-5 Spanish bullets tied to data; malformed JSON stored as raw text
RecommendationContent.tsx renders JSON bullets as a short list with Instagram links, falls back to markdown for legacy prose rows
metrics.compute_highlights gains engagement_drop and posting_gap signals with a severity field
Roster page surfaces a needs-attention indicator per influencer from recent highlights
pytest green including new content-analysis and alert-threshold tests; Playwright covers bullet rendering + fallback + roster badges
features.notes: Spec: agent-harness/spec.md (rewritten 2026-07-07). User confirmed scope, Spanish-only bullets, and full video+audio analysis on 2026-07-07. Merged via PR #1 (2026-07-07).

features.name: Visual redesign — "Fresh Current" theme
features.goal: Replace the platform's ad-hoc dark neutral/amber theme with a single deliberate light visual identity ("Fresh Current"): cool light canvas, white shadowed cards, a teal-to-sky gradient accent used sparingly, Plus Jakarta Sans (display) + Inter (body), and Kolsquare-grade bold/scannable metric presentation.
features.why_it_matters: The prior theme was generic inline neutral-950/amber-500 Tailwind classes with no central tokens, a dead Arial override hiding the loaded fonts, and no distinct visual identity. The agency wants a clean, confident, intuitive dashboard that reads its numbers as clearly as reference tools like Kolsquare.
non_goals: No new pages or features - visual layer only, same data/behavior
No auth/session model changes
No dark mode / theme toggle - single fixed light theme
No changes to scraper/, metrics calculations, or recommendation generation
acceptance_criteria: globals.css defines semantic color tokens (canvas, card, border, ink, muted, accent, accent-2, positive, negative) via Tailwind v4 @theme, replacing all inline neutral-*/amber-*/emerald-*/red-4* literals
Root layout loads Plus Jakarta Sans (--font-display) and Inter (--font-body); body font-family actually uses the loaded font (no more hardcoded Arial override)
Nav, roster, influencer detail, trends, and login pages all use the new tokens; no leftover neutral-*/amber-* classes anywhere in app/
FollowerChart (Recharts) hardcoded hex values updated to the new palette with a gradient line
Login page gets the gradient wordmark + gradient CTA as the direction's signature element
npm run build, npm run lint, and existing Playwright suite (5/5) all pass
scraper pytest suite unaffected (64/64 passing) - confirms no cross-boundary changes
Roster and influencer detail pages visually verified against a running dev server via Playwright screenshots
features.notes: Five directions were mocked up as an HTML artifact and reviewed with the user before build; "Fresh Current" was selected. Spec: agent-harness/spec.md (this entry). Built and verified 2026-07-07.

features.name: Trend-grounded recommendations (real-time Spain feeds)
features.goal: Scrape live, current Spanish trend/news RSS feeds instead of evergreen marketing blogs, and pipe the distilled trend headlines into each influencer's recommendation prompt so bullets name specific shows/people/events.
features.why_it_matters: Recommendations were citing vague placeholders like 'a currently trending Spanish TV show' because (a) the scraped trend sources were static 2026-marketing-advice blogs with no named current events, and (b) trend data was never passed into the recommendation prompt at all despite being scraped daily.
non_goals: No new Supabase tables or schema changes
No new third-party dependencies (stdlib XML parsing only)
No per-influencer trend-source targeting - one shared daily trend list, filtered per persona by Gemini in-prompt
No auth/platform UI/scrape-frequency changes
No influencer-brand matching
acceptance_criteria: config.TREND_SOURCES replaced with 3 verified-live feeds: Google Trends Spain RSS, 20minutos Gente RSS, El Pais Cultura RSS — done
trend_scraper.scrape_trend_source gains an RSS parse branch (stdlib xml.etree.ElementTree), returns same {title, content_text} shape, HTML path unchanged — done
trend_headlines.build_prompt requires headlines to name a specific show/person/event, skips themeless items — done
recommendations.build_prompt/generate_recommendation accept trend_items and add a 'What's trending in Spain today' section instructing named references only — done
run_daily.run_recommendations fetches latest trend_headlines once per run and passes English texts into every generate_recommendation call, gracefully falling back to None — done
pytest green including new/updated RSS parsing, prompt-section, and run_daily wiring tests — done, 125/125

Live run_daily (or targeted manual script) confirms named current items in trend_snapshots/trend_headlines and at least one recommendation bullet naming a specific trend — done: scrape_trend_source run live against all 3 feeds returned named current items (Tom Hanks 70th, Barcelona heatwave, Meghan Markle/Harry, El Pais culture stories); rendered recommendation prompt confirmed to include and correctly instruct on named trend items
features.notes: Spec: agent-harness/spec.md (rewritten for this change 2026-07-10). Supersedes feature_004's 'URL scraping only, no RSS' non-goal now that live current-events sources are needed. Also fixed a stale test_config.py assertion (len==4) left over from feature_004, now len==3. Not run against real Gemini/Supabase this session (no live run_daily against real DB) — trend scraping and prompt rendering verified directly instead.

features.name: Scraper reliability hardening (retry, alerting, anomaly gates)
features.goal: Make the daily Instagram scrape resilient: same-day retry of failed handles, visible alerting on incomplete runs, loud failure on Instagram response-shape drift, anomaly gate before profile writes, and bounded retry with distinct session-expiry handling.
features.why_it_matters: The scraper is the platform's sole data source. On 2026-07-08 all 5 handles failed, the run marked itself complete, never retried, and alerted no one - that day's data is permanently gone. Private-field reads default to 0/None on Instagram shape changes, silently corrupting engagement metrics.
non_goals: No Instagram Graph API migration (Phase 2 - declined)
No third-party scraping APIs or email/webhook infrastructure; no new dependencies
No increase in scrape volume - each handle at most once per day
No restructuring of the per-handle write sequence (audit H3)
No platform/UI staleness indicator (audit M5)
No automatic backfill.py wiring - deep catch-up stays manual
acceptance_criteria: mark_ran_today() only fires when every roster handle has a snapshot today; failed days are re-attempted at 13:00/17:00 launchd fires, skipping already-scraped handles
Incomplete runs log an ERROR naming missing handles and post a macOS notification
_comment_count raises on missing 'comments' key; video with no view key logs a warning
Profile snapshots with followers 0/None or >50% day-over-day swing are rejected before insert
Connection-class errors retried up to 3 attempts with backoff; login/challenge exceptions abort the roster loop with a distinct session-expired alert
pytest green in scraper/ including new tests for each mechanism
